description: Geopolitical zone voting patterns 2011–2023. Based on states-won summaries — not full vote-share tables.
notes: All data marked provenance_type='inferred_pattern' — derived from states-won summaries, NOT from full state-level vote tables. Treat as priors only.

data.pattern_2011_2023: Predominantly voted for northern Muslim candidates. 2011: Jonathan (PDP) won; 2015: Buhari (APC) dominant; 2019: Buhari dominant; 2023: Split — Tinubu (APC) won most states but NNPP's Kwankwaso won Kano.

Predominantly APC/Buhari stronghold 2015-2019. Borno and Yobe very high APC margins. Adamawa competitive (PDP governor in 2023). Security crisis (Boko Haram/ISWAP) affects turnout in Borno, Yobe, Adamawa.
Middle Belt — historically competitive. FCT voted for Peter Obi (LP) in 2023 — significant break from pattern. Plateau is PDP-leaning. Benue is PDP-leaning. Kogi APC.
Tinubu/APC stronghold 2015-2023. Lagos is the critical prize. 2023: Tinubu won SW but Peter Obi ran strongly in Lagos urban areas. PDP's Makinde holds Oyo. PDP won Osun 2022.
Heavily voted Igbo candidates. 2023: Peter Obi (LP) dominated; APGA holds Anambra. APC held Ebonyi and Imo. Historically PDP stronghold when no Igbo candidate running.
PDP stronghold historically. 2023: Mostly PDP in governorships; Rivers under Fubara (PDP). Significant political tension in Rivers 2024-2026 (Fubara vs. Wike).
